Document:
# Paramedic SOP This is the list of procedures, responsibilities, and duties of the [color=#42636d][bold]Paramedic[/bold][/color] of this station.
1. The Paramedic is expected to fulfill the duties of regular [color=#42636d][bold]Medical Doctors[/bold][/color] if there are no active Medical Doctors/Interns. In addition, when treating personnel, the Paramedic is expected to follow the same SOP as Medical Doctors.
2. Paramedics should focus on tending to personnel that are injured outside of Medbay and are otherwise unable to swiftly come to Medbay to be examined and treated. In the case that the patient is in critical condition, it is recommended that the Paramedic first remove the patient from immediate danger, then stabilize the patient before bringing them to Medbay for proper treatment.
3. In the highly unlikely event of tending to personnel in an active combat zone, the Paramedic should refrain from rescuing patients if rescuing them would place the Paramedic in immediate and obvious danger. Focus should instead be placed on taking advantage of opportunities to rescue those who can be safely extracted, stabilizing all of them, then shuttling them to Medbay. If the combat zone moves towards the Paramedic’s triage area, the Paramedic should instead focus on relocating downed personnel to a safe location or to Medbay, if possible.
4. When possible, the Paramedic should communicate where they need access to so that either appropriate staff or the station’s AI can let them through. However, in cases of extreme emergency, Paramedics are permitted to use whatever means available to rescue injured personnel so long as excessive station damage is not incurred. Other station personnel will be more than happy to help Paramedics save lives. A Paramedic should only consider acquiring extra access if a confirmed threat to the station is identified and Blue Alert or higher is called.
5. When not out on call, the Paramedic should assist in monitoring station personnel health remotely via Crew Monitor. It is highly recommended to encourage crew to turn on their suits’ integrated coordinates; however, they are not required to have coordinates on if the current alert level is below Red.
